response.justification: Strong general software engineering fundamentals with proven experience architecting complex systems, but critical technical misalignment: minimal Java exposure (no Kotlin), zero Spring ecosystem knowledge, and no demonstrated IDE tooling or language internals experience. Recent career trajectory (5+ years) has been web-focused rather than JVM/enterprise tooling, making this a significant capability gap for a mid-senior IDE role.
strengths: Proven ability to design and architect complex, large-scale systems (microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, low-latency infrastructure)
Strong software craftsmanship practices and open-source contributions (multiple published libraries and tools) demonstrating knowledge sharing and depth
Demonstrated rapid language and framework learning across diverse ecosystems (D, TypeScript, Node.js, C++, showing adaptability)
concerns: Minimal Java experience (mentioned only twice in 15+ years of roles) with no Kotlin expertise whatsoever—core to the position
Zero Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise framework experience despite these being key technical focus areas
No IDE development or language tooling background; brief 2014-2015 LabWindows/CVI exposure is outdated; recent 5-year trajectory is web development (TypeScript, React, full-stack)
